title: austrostipa mundula
intro: Austrostipa mundula, also known as the blue grass, is a perennial grass species native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Austrostipa mundula are thought to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Austrostipa mundula can be prepared as a tea or infusion, with some herbal practitioners also using it as a tincture or in a topical cream application.
